name: start-work
tagline: Autonomous Codex orchestrator that dispatches subagents to execute dev plans and merges PRs to main by default.
verdict: Detailed, internally consistent orchestration protocol with real strengths: strict verification gates, an evidence ledger, and an explicit failure-retry loop. But by default it merges PRs into the main branch without a per-merge confirmation (only a prior opt-out), and it assumes Codex-only subagent tools (multi_agent_v1/spawn_agent, lazycodex-worker roles)

highlights.summary: Line 172: 'merge by default unless explicitly opted out' — auto-merges PR to main branch with no per-merge user confirmation, only a standing opt-out.
Description promises 'worktree discipline' but the body (line 172) extends that to autonomously creating/merging PRs to main — a consequential action not flagged in the short description.
Line 108 falls back to a third-party GitHub tool ('agent-browser', vercel-labs) with no version pin or integrity/verification guidance.
